scope_note: Johns Hopkins University does not publish a minimum GPA cutoff or admitted-student GPA distribution on the cited official pages used here. The policy section stays official; the estimate card below is third-party and directional.
policy_summary: Johns Hopkins does not publish a minimum GPA on the cited official admissions pages. Hopkins says it reviews applications holistically in the context of a student's school and recommends a challenging high school curriculum rather than a fixed GPA cutoff.

what_mit_does_say.claim: Johns Hopkins does not publish a minimum GPA on the cited official admissions pages. Hopkins says it reviews applications holistically in the context of a student's school and recommends a challenging high school curriculum rather than a fixed GPA cutoff.

recommended_gpa.summary: Johns Hopkins does not publish HS GPA data for its incoming class on the cited official pages. The estimate below is a third-party directional reference, not an official Hopkins cutoff.

estimates.label: Weighted (estimate)
estimates.method: Estimated by PrepScholar because the university did not officially report an average incoming high-school GPA on the cited admissions pages.

recommended_gpa.interpretation: This estimate points to an applicant pool with very strong grades, but Hopkins' official process remains holistic and school-context driven. Treat the number as directional rather than required.
